data.title: Ju.com launched its new product matrix, Hyper Trade. Within 24 hours of its launch, Hyper Trade saw over 10,000 participants and more than 200,000 orders.
data.description: On April 23, Ju.com released data for the first day of its new product matrix, Hyper Trade. Within 24 hours of its launch, the series of products had accumulated over 10,000 participants and more than 200,000 orders. Among them, "Bounty Battle," "Fingertip Trading," and "Fun BTC" were the most popular products. The official statement indicated that Hyper Trade's low barrier to entry and high transparency attract users to participate in the lightweight market prediction field.

data.title: UK Bitcoin Accumulator Faces Pressure to Liquidate Holdings
data.description: Shareholders of a UK-based Bitcoin accumulator are urging the company to sell all its cryptocurrency holdings and cease operations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the growing pressure from investors who are concerned about the current market conditions and the future prospects of the cryptocurrency sector. The shareholders believe that liquidating the Bitcoin assets could be a prudent move given the volatility and uncertainties surrounding the digital currency market. This development reflects a broader trend of caution among investors in the cryptocurrency space, as they navigate the challenges posed by fluctuating prices and regulatory changes.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Evaluation of Three Image Generation Models
data.description: Three image generation models were tested across various dimensions. According to PANews, the evaluation included interesting prompts for generating images. The models were assessed for their performance and capabilities in producing visual content. The results provide insights into the strengths and weaknesses of each model in handling different types of image generation tasks.

data.title: Baidu to Hold Board Meeting for Q1 2026 Results Approval
data.description: Baidu has announced that it will hold a board meeting on May 18, 2026, to approve its unaudited financial results for the three months ending March 31, 2026. According to Jin10, the meeting will focus on reviewing and announcing the company's performance for the first quarter of the year.

data.title: GoPlus AI Launches AgentGuard Checkup to Enhance AI Security
data.description:
GoPlus AI has officially launched the Checkup feature for its AgentGuard security component, aiming to address security blind spots in AI agents' autonomous decision-making and asset control. According to Foresight News, this feature elevates AI agent security from fragmented interception to systematic measurement.

The Checkup function establishes a six-dimensional evaluation system covering code, keys, runtime, Web3, configuration, and trust chains. It provides developers with a visual health report, complete with Tier ratings and repair suggestions, within 30 seconds.

Additionally, Checkup has strengthened Web3 defense rules by incorporating built-in wallet drainer and unlimited authorization detection, setting stringent security compliance standards for AI agents involved in financial asset flows. The GoPlus team emphasized that "trust is the cornerstone of large-scale commercial use of AI agents," and Checkup aims to establish a trust foundation in the AI era through a quantifiable and visual health check mechanism, ensuring each agent is certified for operation.

data.title: South Korea's Financial Services Commission Rejects KIDI's Expansion Proposal
data.description: South Korea's Financial Services Commission has declined KIDI's proposal to venture into digital asset and AI sectors. According to NS3.AI, KIDI submitted an amendment request on February 9, aiming to issue educational tokens and establish an AI subsidiary. The commission's decision reflects concerns that such expansion might be premature in the absence of a foundational digital asset law.

data.title: Britain's Private Sector Rebounds After Iran Conflict Slowdown
data.description: Britain's private sector has swiftly recovered from the recent slowdown attributed to the Iran war, according to a closely monitored survey. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the resilience of the sector amid geopolitical tensions. The survey indicates a positive turnaround, suggesting that businesses have adapted to the challenges posed by the conflict. Analysts are optimistic about the sector's continued growth, despite ongoing uncertainties in the global landscape. The report underscores the importance of strategic planning and adaptability in navigating complex international situations.

data.title: RootData Urges Web3 Projects to Enhance Transparency
data.description:
RootData, a Web3 asset data platform, has issued a transparency alert on Twitter (X), highlighting missing information from projects such as Mars Protocol, Anvil, Lynex, AstridDAO, and Hatom Labs. According to ChainCatcher, the platform noted the absence of team details, key calendar events, and token data, urging these projects to submit or update their information on RootData to improve their transparency scores.

RootData's transparency score is designed to assess the completeness and timeliness of a project's information disclosure. The scoring system ranges from A to F, with lower scores indicating less comprehensive information disclosure and higher risk of misconduct. Investors are advised to exercise caution with projects that have lower transparency scores.

data.title: UK Businesses Report Record Cost Increases Amid Inflation Concerns
data.description: On April 23, PMI survey results indicated that a growing number of UK businesses are reporting rising costs, marking a historic increase. According to Jin10, this trend suggests higher inflation in the future as the impact of the Iran war spreads across the economy. The composite PMI input price index saw its largest increase in 28 years, reaching its highest level since double-digit inflation emerged at the end of 2022. This survey may heighten concerns for the Bank of England that significant energy price hikes could lead to broader inflation expectations. However, the extent to which deteriorating labor market conditions can mitigate these effects remains a critical question.

data.title: STOCKS | U.S. Software Stocks Decline Pre-Market Following IBM's Weak Earnings
data.description: U.S. software stocks experienced a decline in pre-market trading after IBM reported weak earnings. According to Jin10, Microsoft shares fell by 1.5%, Adobe saw a decrease of 2.1%, and CrowdStrike dropped by 2.4%.

data.title: Alibaba Cloud Launches Enterprise-Level AI Platform JVS Crew
data.description:
Alibaba Cloud has introduced a new enterprise-level intelligent platform named JVS Crew. According to ChainCatcher, the platform is designed with the concept of 'integration,' allowing businesses to seamlessly embed it into existing applications, SaaS services, or smart hardware. This enables companies to quickly access production-grade AI agent capabilities without barriers.

JVS Crew handles 80% of platform-level tasks such as multi-tenant isolation, security compliance, cost accounting, and channel access. It utilizes built-in atomic APIs and SDKs to enhance existing products with intelligent capabilities, facilitating a transition from individual to mass production. The platform is now available on Alibaba Cloud's official website, where enterprise users and developers can explore product details and apply for testing.

data.title: Trader Murad's Portfolio Recovers Significantly
data.description: Arkham reports that trader Murad's portfolio has rebounded to approximately $12 million, marking a 50% increase from its previous low of $7.8 million. According to Odaily, despite this recovery, the portfolio remains about 82% below its historical peak. During this period, Murad did not sell his holdings in SPX and other meme coins.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Drops Below 78,000 USDT with a 0.07% Decrease in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 23, 2026, 08:25 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has dropped below 78,000 USDT and is now trading at 77,990.578125 USDT, with a narrowed 0.07% decrease in 24 hours.

data.title: Iranian Parliament Deputy Speaker: First revenue from Strait of Hormuz toll fees has been deposited.
data.description: The Deputy Speaker of the Iranian Parliament stated that the first payment from the Strait of Hormuz toll revenue has been deposited into the Central Bank of Iran's account. (Tasnim News Agency, Iran)

data.title: Murad's portfolio has recovered to $12 million, but is still down 82% from its peak.
data.description: Arkham reported that trader Murad's portfolio has recovered to approximately $12 million, up about 50% from its previous low of $7.8 million, but still down about 82% from its all-time high. Murad did not sell any SPX or other Meme tokens during this period.

data.title: South Korea Expands Debt Assessment to Include Virtual Assets
data.description: South Korea is set to enhance its debt assessment procedures by incorporating checks on debtors' financial and virtual asset holdings. According to NS3.AI, the Financial Services Commission announced that a revision to the Credit Information Use and Protection Act has been approved by the National Assembly. Previously, repayment evaluations primarily considered real estate and tax information. This amendment aims to address calls for more comprehensive asset evaluations to mitigate moral hazard.

data.title: OKX will launch OPGUSDT perpetual contracts.
data.description: OKX announced that it will officially launch the OPGUSDT perpetual contract on its website, app, and API at 17:00 (UTC+8) on April 23, 2026. According to available information, OpenGradient is a decentralized AI infrastructure used to host, execute, and validate models.

data.title: Euro Area Business Activity Contracts Amid Iran Conflict Impact
data.description: Business activity in the euro area has unexpectedly contracted for the first time since late 2024, driven by a significant decline in the services sector. Bloomberg posted on X that the ongoing conflict in Iran is exerting pressure on consumer sentiment, contributing to the downturn. The contraction marks a notable shift in the region's economic landscape, highlighting the broader implications of geopolitical tensions on market dynamics. Analysts are closely monitoring the situation as it unfolds, assessing potential impacts on future economic performance in the eurozone.

data.title: U.S. Awaits Iran's Response to Resume Peace Talks
data.description: The United States has announced that it is awaiting a response from Iran to move forward with peace negotiations between conflicting parties. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the ongoing diplomatic efforts to resolve tensions. The U.S. government has expressed its readiness to engage in discussions, contingent upon Iran's willingness to participate. This development comes amid heightened geopolitical tensions and efforts to stabilize the region. The U.S. aims to facilitate dialogue and foster a peaceful resolution, emphasizing the importance of cooperation from all involved parties.

data.title: Global Crypto Market Faces Volatility Amid Geopolitical and Policy Uncertainties
data.description: In April, the global cryptocurrency market experienced fluctuations influenced by geopolitical tensions between the U.S. and Iran, a leadership crisis at the Federal Reserve, and the listing of Morgan Stanley's Bitcoin ETF. According to PANews, Bitcoin demonstrated unique resilience during this period. However, the combination of geopolitical and policy uncertainties has made it challenging for the market to find a consensus on short-term direction.
